page: UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, offering 48 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, translating to 5.33 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with 2048 GB of SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring in the bottom 10% and single-threaded gzip compression in the bottom 25%. However, multi-threaded execution is robust; the server achieves strong results in the top 25% for bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, PassMark CPU score, CPU extended instructions, and CPU encryption tests. Memory bandwidth remains average across both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks.

Given its high resource density of 48 cores and 256 GB RAM paired with 2 TB of local SSD storage, this server offers a balanced hardware profile for multi-threaded workloads. It is well-suited for parallel processing tasks, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring high single-core clock speeds.
description: The UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is a general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with an AMD EPYC 7542 processor featuring 48 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and a 2.0 GHz clock speed under a shared CPU allocation model. The system includes 256.0 GB of RAM and 2048 GB of local SSD storage. Benchmark data reveals a performance tradeoff: single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression, rank in the lower percentiles, whereas multi-threaded operations like bzip3 decompression, PassMark CPU scores, and encryption tests perform in the top 25%. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from high core counts and substantial memory, rather than single-threaded processing.
og_description: UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is a general-purpose KVM virtual server featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2048 GB local SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
meta_description: UpCloud 48xCPU-256GB is a KVM virtual server with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2048 GB SSD, built for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A multi-core virtual server combining forty-eight AMD EPYC cores with substantial memory and local SSD storage for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 48 AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs with a 2.0 GHz clock speed
256 GB of system memory providing 5.33 GB per core
2048 GB of bundled local SSD storage
Strong multi-threaded performance in decompression and encryption benchmarks
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and parallel processing
Lower relative performance on single-threaded tasks
